About.Name: Jake R Graham
About.AboutText: My name is Jake Graham; I am a dedicated and hard-working Electrical Engineering student at Seattle Pacific University.

Experience.ExperienceText: Beyond my education, I serve in a minor role on my church's tech team, where I alternately run the lights or the broadcast audio for the service. I also routinely perform repairs on my house, its appliances, and my car, including changing batteries and caliper pins on my car, installing new power outlets, changing batteries on laptops, and other such necessities.
Experience.EducationText: I have an aptitude for mathematics, computer science, and engineering. Because of these, I was highly encouraged throughout my life to pursue an engineering degree. I initially resisted the idea when I graduated from High School because I didn't particularly enjoy either the experience or the classes, but after taking a year off I was encouraged to continue my education, and to my delight, I found that all of the interesting classes I had been wanting were found in Higher Education. Since that discovery, I have been working and studying hard to get an Electrical Engineering degree in order to become an Embedded Engineer, where I hope to use my math, computer science, and engineering skills to create new solutions for complex problems.

Project1.Title: 10 kHz Sallen-Key Filter
Project1.Desc:
A project I completed in my Circuits II class was to design a 10kHz Bandpass Sallen-Key filter whose output was put into a Peak Detector and Comparator,
so that a signal was output from the comparator only when a 10kHz signal was put into the filter. I designed a 4-stage filter with a Quality factor of 4 for each stage for a maximum amount of attenuation,
and I used a voltage divider at the input of the circuit to reduce the filter's gain by a factor of 1000V/V. Below are pictures of the filter, the filter schematic, and a Frequency-Response Analysis Bode Plot of the filter.
